records.ety: [L. impedire, lit., to entangle the feet; pref. im- in + pes, pedis, foot. See Foot, and cf. Impeach.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Impeded; p. pr. & vb. n. Impeding.]

defs: To hinder; to stop in progress; to obstruct; as, to impede the advance of troops.
quotes: Whatever hinders or impedes The action of the nobler will.
